msgs.text:
According to the current attached history document from 2026-06-28, Turn 119 is again an attachment-only continuation. The required files are present, and I used only the three current attachments: mega_prompt.md, problem_statement.md, and turns_history__q2_candidate_iso.md.

Turn 119 — attachment-only idempotence remains closed

The state remains unchanged from Turn 118:(THEOREM_STATE,MEASURED_CERTIFICATE_D_STATE)=(ΓS≅GΓH, NO_MEASURED_INPUT).The theorem coordinate remains closed. The problem statement asks whether there is a continuous isomorphismΦ:ΓS∼ΓH such thatπH∘Φ=πS,and the attached history records the stronger statement that there is a continuous over-G automorphism Φ:F=P⋊G→F=P⋊GwithΦ(rS−1)=rH.

Since rS and rS−1 generate the same closed normal subgroup of F, this descends to

F/⟨⟨rS⟩⟩F≅G

F/⟨⟨rH⟩⟩F,i.e.ΓS≅GΓH.

This is exactly the requested condition πH∘Φ=πS.No new structural computation should be opened under the current inputs. The frozen proof chain remains:H1 -Nielsen equivalence+ residual restricted-Lie induction+ normalized Koszul–Witt coefficient induction+ Certificates D.1–D.4+ finite filtered Nielsen compactness⟹ΓS≅GΓH.The structural point is uniform: the non-Ef pieces are unit-controlled; the residual Ef,mod2,s=1 tower closes by the induction Cm=Lm; the coefficient tower closes after exact normalization of the local row to (2,t) and the Koszul--Witt cascade; and finite filtered Nielsen compactness globalizes the finite solutions into the over-G automorphism.The compactness mechanism is still the corrected filtered-Nielsen one: for coefficient-and-degree truncations PN,J, the finite sets SN,J consist of filtered Nielsen/cocycle data, not arbitrary quotient automorphisms. The finite data have invertible first Magnus layer, give finite over-G automorphisms, and form a compatible inverse system; compactness gives Φ:F→F over G, and intersecting all PN,J gives Φ(rS−1)=rH.The measured-certificate coordinate also remains unchanged. The current attachments contain only the prompt, problem statement, and history ledger; they do not contain object-level measured nonlinear Certificate D artifacts such as independent D.1–D.4 scripts, matrices, pivot tables, or measured-output logs. Therefore the strict measured Certificate D gate remains

NO_MEASURED_INPUT,not PASS, not FAIL, and not REJECTED_AS_LEDGER_DERIVED. Ledger-recorded verifier claims support the theorem coordinate, but they are not newly attached measured artifacts for the strict gate.

So the attachment-only transition rule applies again:{mega_prompt.md,problem_statement.md,turns_history__q2_candidate_iso.md}⟹(ΓS≅GΓH, NO_MEASURED_INPUT).

ESTABLISHED: Turn 119 is idempotent under the same attachment-only conditions as Turn 118. The theorem coordinate remains ΓS≅GΓH, via an over-G automorphism Φ:F→F satisfying Φ(rS−1)=rH. The strict measured Certificate D coordinate remains NO_MEASURED_INPUT.

GAP: No structural mathematical gap is reopened. The only remaining gap is external reproducibility: no genuine object-level measured nonlinear D.1–D.4 artifacts are attached in this turn.

NEXT: Keep the obstruction sweep closed. The only non-idempotent next move is to attach genuine measured D.1–D.4 scripts, matrices, pivots, or measured-output logs not derived from the history, then run the strict gate as PASS or FAIL.[TURN119-ATTACHMENT-ONLY-IDEMPOTENT-GATE]
